quote_of_day.text:
>>11390
Dead internet theory feels more real every day. People are tired of AI-generated superficial content they see on social media. Imageboards/Chan culture is the last place left on the internet for genuine, unfiltered conversation.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of moderator intervention moved over the board, bringing scattered bans and deletions. A thread about fagyush's alleged ban for posting an image.

A high-pressure ridge of community discussion formed in the afternoon, with one anon pondering the migration of the Indiachan community. The topic., with some questioning the relevance of imageboards.

A brief but intense downpour of ridicule occurred when an anon invited friends to eat and got roasted, collecting 17 responses.

Meanwhile, a scattered drizzle of personal updates and boasts also occurred, including an anon sharing a daily workout routine to become a 'chad', and another questioning a couple's looks, each drawing 17 responses.

articles.title: Anon Claims Fagyush Banned for Posting Image
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread claiming that fagyush was deleted and banned for creating a thread with an image. One anon expressed frustration with another user, saying they were full of hate and questioning what they hoped to achieve with their behavior.

This anon listed several groups the original poster allegedly hated, including "tymyies, slims, pakees, deleets, jo0s, s0uthies, baamaans, kanglues." Another anon shared their experience with posting content, specifically anti-Muslim material, and claimed to get 100k-1M views.

They described it as their hobby and found the reach to be "hilarious and insane." A respondent criticized a user for their stance against Modi, calling it "very low IQ." They praised Modi as "objectively a great leader" and expressed a desire for him to be more dictatorial and anti-Muslim.

One user revealed having multiple accounts aimed at lowering the threshold for Indian Hindus towards gore and violence against Muslims. They framed this as their "contribution to saving the nation." The conversation also involved personal insults and vulgarities.

Anon at one point described a graphic genital mutilation scenario. Some users engaged in direct confrontation, with one claiming to be in Bangalore and offering to lynch another user they believed to be Muslim.

The thread included various exchanges of a personal and insulting nature, with one user linking to a profile and another implying a user's geographical location.

articles.title: Anon Ponders Migration of Indiachan Community
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread questioning the future of Indiachan, suggesting that imageboards feel 'niche' and 'encourage cultural stagnation.' They wondered where the community would migrate if Indiachan were to die.

Another anon responded that traditionalists would likely move to Instagram or Reddit, while 'librandoo lurkers' would leave due to the unfiltered slurs. This anon also mentioned that Xitter already exists for those who would traditionally go to chans.

A different anon suggested using the 8chan version of Indiachan and expressed frustration with the current state of the site, citing a past incident where a joke led to a threat of the site being taken down.

In response to a comment about the site dying soon, one anon simply stated that it was the poster's time to leave, not the site.

The conversation also touched on the 'dead internet theory,' with an anon stating that people are tired of superficial content on social media and that imageboards are one of the last places for genuine conversation.

The discussion also devolved into the typical back-and-forth, with one anon listing various groups they claimed were 'bad' and another making a comment about caste.

One respondent did offer some perspective, noting that Indiachan is new and that it takes time for communities to build up.

They pointed out that old users often leave and new ones join, which happens in every iteration of these types of sites.

articles.title: Anon invites friends to eat, gets roasted
articles.body:
Anon started a thread inviting friends to eat, posting 'yum yum khane aajao frens'. One respondent called Anon 'Gross yaar southie' and told them to stop eating so much rice, or they would get a potbelly.

Anon replied that they were not from the south. Another anon asked Anon to post a picture of their body. A separate poster made a comment about Dravidians, to which Anon responded that they were not one.

The thread took a turn for the absurd when an anon asked Anon to post their body, and another anon made a bizarre comment about having '1 dudu small 1 dudu big'.

Some posters hurled insults at Anon, calling them a 'literal murderer' and 'litreal rapist'. Anon also received a few kind responses, with one poster saying 'Thanks pyaare' and another saying 'Thanks'.

Amidst the chaos, one anon simply asked 'why yaara', and another posted 'BDL is showing'. The thread descended into typical board chatter with little coherent discussion.

articles.title: Anon shares daily workout routine, claims to become 'chad'
articles.body:
Anon posted a daily workout routine consisting of push-ups, decline push-ups, archer squats, pull-ups, and pike push-ups, claiming it would help them 'ascend and become a chad.' Another anon questioned the routine, pointing out that it lacked exercises for abs, calves, forearms, and love handles/obliques.

The original poster replied that they would add leg raises for abs and calf raises with weights for calves. Anon also received comments on the effectiveness of bodyweight exercises and their own physical attributes.

articles.title: Anon Questions Looksmaxxers on Couple
articles.body:
Anon posted a link to an NDTV article about a car accident in Bengaluru, asking how 'looksmaxxers' would explain the couple involved.

One anon replied that the OP thinks the man is not a 'pimp & rand manager' and the woman is not a 'professional rand.' Anon also joked about the couple's caste, with one saying the woman's parents should have 'honor killed' her.

The thread included various comments, including 'kek' and 'pasmanda boys pimp syeda girls.'
